headline: Washington's resentment toward 'paper tiger' NATO is growing
description: The Trump administration has accused NATO allies of failing to show solidarity with the United States during the war with Iran. Its frustration is particularly strong toward Spain, France and the United Kingdom.

headline: 'Everything seemed to be cracking and melting': Former 'liquidator' recounts Chernobyl disaster, 40 years later
Notorious burglar 'Le Chat' detained over Paris heists totaling €10 million
French hostages say they need more support after returning: 'Coming back was almost as hard as being detained. I felt isolated'
description: For many former French hostages and detainees held abroad, the ordeal continues upon their return to France, marked by a loss of bearings and a sense of abandonment.

headline: Driven by women and solo travelers, small group tours attract renewed interest
description: Despite the war in the Middle East, small group travel has seen a resurgence in recent years. Altaï, one of the major French players in the sector, announced it would be acquired by Intrepid Travel, the Australian company that is one of the global leaders in the market.

headline: French football's new anti-discrimination campaign is criticized
description: This weekend, Ligue 1 players are wearing jerseys bearing the first names of individuals who have experienced discrimination. The new initiative replaces the yearly rainbow-flag campaign against homophobia.

headline: 'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
description: In an interview, Lawrence Vale, professor of urban planning at MIT, explains how the American president is striving to leave his mark on the federal capital through initiatives such as the Triumphal Arch, White House ballroom and statues.

headline: West Bank holds municipal elections under tight control of Palestinian Authority and Israel
French teen gets 15-year jail term for killing teacher
Claude Bessy, star dancer and director of Paris Opéra's dance school, dies at 93
Google to invest up to $40 billion in AI firm Anthropic
Anger at funeral of Lebanese journalist killed by Israeli army: 'Amal gave voice to the south and the sacrifices endured by this region'
Venezuela, Colombia pledge military cooperation on first post-Maduro visit
In Moldova, families gather at cemeteries for 'Easter of the Dead'
Two riot police officers prosecuted for off-duty assault in Nice
French government unveils urgent bill with measures on urban renewal and energy-inefficient homes
Five spots to bite into unforgettable madeleines
Ban on toxic chemicals stalled by EU's 'limited progress'
How tulips took over the world
How Iranian propaganda lures the West into distraction
description: Pro-Iranian accounts are flooding the internet with AI-generated satirical videos that exploit Lego's popularity and Donald Trump's unpopularity, in an attempt to divert Western attention from the suffering of the Iranian people.

headline: 'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
description: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

headline: In Lebanon, Israel establishes an uninhabited buffer zone behind a 'yellow line'
description: The 'demarcation line' drawn by Israel on the third day of the ceasefire has revived memories of the 18-year occupation of Lebanon from 1982 to 2000. The Israeli army has banned Lebanese citizens from entering this 'defense zone,' which covers 6% of the country.

headline: French Socialists' policy platform: Neither new nor complete, but at least it exists
'Some French businesses are willing to associate with the Rassemblement National, which harbors a deep mistrust of labor unions'
'Sudan, devastated by two warlords, faces the world's worst humanitarian crisis'
